title: gastrolobium parviflorum
intro: Gastrolobium parviflorum, also known as poison, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to cause vomiting and purging, which can help to treat conditions such as indigestion and diarrhea. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, Gastrolobium parviflorum is often used as a natural remedy in the form of tinctures or infusions, although it's essential to approach its use with caution due to its potent toxic properties.
